books.title: Mapping the World with Art
books.author: Ellen Johnston McHenry
books.rationale: This book is a blend of history, geography, and art. The text is about the history of cartography, from the earliest maps that were drawn on clay by the people of Mesopotamia to the exploration of the "new world."  This book also includes map drawing lessons that take students step by step through the process of drawing maps of all the countries mentioned in the text.

books.title: Canadian Student Atlas
books.author: Collins Maps
books.rationale: Used across the curriculum as a reference and as a text to read in some lessons. With interesting information and pictures, this is an economical reference for students.

books.title: Exploring the Frozen North
books.author: Pierre Berton
books.rationale: Pierre Berton's prose style and engaging narrative histories inspired generations of Canadians to treasure their nation's stories, and this compendium collects four of his books for younger readers in a single volume. The unknown coasts of the Arctic and the long-fabled Northwest Passage route to Asia loomed large in the nineteenth-century imagination, and this book introduces middle-grade readers to four important historical figures who shaped our modern knowledge of this region: William Edward Parry, Jane Franklin (Sir John Franklin's widow), Elisha Kent Kane, and Robert John McClure.
